prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes stock prices follow a random walk with a drift. Historical daily price data for MA from the last 5 years was used to estimate the annualized drift (15.94%) and volatility (24.19%). A Monte Carlo simulation with 10000 paths was performed to project the price in 18 trading days. The point estimate is the median of these simulated prices, and the confidence intervals represent the corresponding percentiles of the distribution. This model assumes historical trends and volatility will continue into the future and does not account for sudden market changes, company-specific news, or macroeconomic factors.
